stories.headline: Marcos issues proclamation convening Congress for special session on June 17
stories.summary: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. signed Proclamation No. 1318 on June 15 calling Congress into a special session on June 17 to act on nine priority measures and to address urgent needs of Mindanao earthquake survivors. The measures include amendments to education and health assistance laws, an anti-political dynasty proposal, and social protection programs. All nine bills have already passed the House and await Senate action.
stories.context: The Senate has been split over a leadership dispute, with Acting Senate President Sherwin Gatchalian leading a new majority bloc and former Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano contesting the changeover. The new majority says it has the votes to conduct business.
stories.watch_for: Congress convenes the special session on June 17 to take up the nine priority bills.

stories.headline: House prosecutors list more than 30 witnesses as Sara Duterte impeachment pre-trial nears
stories.summary: The House prosecution panel filed its pre-trial brief on June 15 containing a list of more than 30 witnesses for Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial, lead prosecutor Gerville Luistro said. The brief centers on allegations of unexplained wealth and non-disclosure of assets in Duterte's statement of assets, liabilities, and net worth. Some witness identities may be withheld for security reasons.
stories.context: The Senate, sitting as an impeachment court, directed both sides to submit pre-trial briefs by June 15. Acting Senate President Sherwin Gatchalian set the pre-trial conference for June 18 at the Senate in Pasay City.
stories.watch_for: The pre-trial conference is set for June 18. The trial proper is scheduled to begin on July 6.

stories.headline: Visayas power grid placed on yellow alert for evening hours amid coal plant outages
stories.summary: The National Grid Corporation of the Philippines declared a yellow alert on the Visayas grid from 4 p.m. to 9 p.m. on June 15, warning that available capacity was insufficient to meet peak demand with adequate contingency reserves. The alert was caused by higher system demand combined with the continued forced outages of several coal power plants, including units of Therma Visayas and Panay Energy Development Corp. Twenty-eight plants across the region are experiencing outages or reduced capacity.
stories.context: Multiple Visayas coal units have experienced extended forced outages since as far back as 2021. A yellow alert signals insufficient operating reserves to cover a major grid contingency, raising the risk of rotational brownouts.

stories.headline: Mindanao earthquake death toll rises to 65 with more than 624,000 people affected
stories.summary: The Office of Civil Defense reported that the death toll from the magnitude 7.8 earthquake that struck off Sarangani on June 8 rose to 65 as of June 15, with about 1,400 injured and 36 still missing. Roughly 624,000 people across 432 barangays remain affected, and infrastructure damage was estimated at P1.13 billion. The OCD said most deaths were caused by earthquake-induced landslides and falling debris.
stories.context: About 14,200 people are sheltering in 37 evacuation centers, with an estimated 57,000 more displaced outside formal facilities. Relief operations are ongoing in hard-hit areas including Glan in Sarangani and General Santos City.

stories.headline: Ateneo coach Tab Baldwin fails to appear at CIDG probe into players' drowning deaths
stories.summary: Coach Tab Baldwin and two other Ateneo de Manila University basketball officials did not personally appear before the Criminal Investigation and Detection Group on June 15 for a fact-finding session on the drowning deaths of players Rene Baterbonia and Divine Adili during a team outing in Aurora on June 8. Only athletics director Em Fernandez appeared personally; the others sent legal representatives. Interior Secretary Remulla said subpoenas will be reissued and non-compliance could lead to contempt charges.
stories.watch_for: The CIDG said it will reissue subpoenas to Baldwin and the other officials who sent representatives. Subpoenas will also be extended to players present at the Aurora outing.

stories.headline: Senator dela Rosa's camp turns over 20 firearms to PNP after license revocation
stories.summary: The Philippine National Police confirmed that 20 firearms belonging to Senator Ronald dela Rosa were surrendered to authorities on June 12 in Davao City through his legal representative and are now under PNP custody. The PNP's Firearms and Explosives Office revoked dela Rosa's license to possess firearms in May following the issuance of an International Criminal Court warrant against him for his alleged role in the Duterte administration's war on drugs.
stories.context: Dela Rosa served as PNP chief from 2016 to 2018 and was a key figure in the Duterte drug war. The FEO revoked licenses for 117 firearms registered to him; 20 have so far been turned over.
stories.watch_for: The remaining 97 firearms registered to dela Rosa that have not yet been surrendered remain subject to the FEO revocation order.

stories.headline: BSP Monetary Board to weigh rate hike as inflation eases but growth slows
stories.summary: Most economists surveyed expect the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas to raise its policy rate by 25 basis points to 4.75 percent at its June 18 meeting, though two institutions forecast a larger increase. Inflation eased to 6.8 percent in May from a three-year high of 7.2 percent in April, but first-quarter gross domestic product growth slowed to 2.8 percent, its weakest in five years, leaving the central bank weighing price stability against economic support.
stories.watch_for: The BSP Monetary Board announces its rate decision on the afternoon of June 18.

stories.headline: PAGASA: No cyclones in Philippine area; easterlies bring isolated storm risk to eastern Visayas and Mindanao
stories.summary: PAGASA reported no tropical cyclone in the Philippine area of responsibility, with the easterlies serving as the dominant weather system and directly affecting the eastern sections of Visayas and Mindanao. Eastern Visayas, the Dinagat Islands, and coastal Mindanao provinces may see partly cloudy to cloudy skies with isolated rainshowers or thunderstorms. The agency warned that severe thunderstorm activity could produce flash floods or landslides in vulnerable areas.

stories.headline: United States and Iran reach framework deal to end war, with formal signing set for Friday
stories.summary: The United States and Iran agreed on a framework to end more than three months of conflict, including Israeli operations in Lebanon, with the deal to be signed Friday in Switzerland, Al Jazeera reported. President Trump announced an immediate ceasefire across all fronts and authorized an end to the US naval blockade of Iranian ports so the Strait of Hormuz can reopen. A 60-day negotiating window follows to address unresolved issues including Iran's nuclear program and sanctions relief.
stories.context: Qatar, Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, and Turkey helped mediate the negotiations. Iran holds uranium enriched to 60 percent purity, near weapons-grade levels; the fate of that stockpile was not addressed in the framework.
stories.watch_for: Vice President JD Vance is expected to attend the formal signing ceremony in Geneva on Friday. Full text of the agreement is to be released within two days.

stories.headline: Asian stock markets surge and oil prices fall as Iran peace deal eases global energy fears
stories.summary: Japanese and South Korean markets led sharp gains across Asia on June 15 following the US-Iran peace announcement, with Japan's Nikkei average approaching 70,000 and South Korea's Kospi closing about 4.6 percent higher, Nikkei Asia reported. Oil prices fell on expectations that the Strait of Hormuz would reopen after months of disruption to roughly a fifth of global oil supply. Analysts cautioned that energy prices could remain elevated for months as risk premiums gradually unwind.

stories.headline: G7 leaders gather in France with Iran deal, Ukraine war, and trade tensions on the agenda
stories.summary: Leaders of the Group of Seven and the European Union gathered in Evian-les-Bains, France, for a three-day summit through June 17 focused on the newly announced US-Iran peace framework, Russia's war in Ukraine, trade disputes, and competition with China, Al Jazeera reported. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi were among the leaders invited to join select working sessions.
stories.context: France holds the G7 rotating presidency in 2026. The summit drew large protests in nearby Geneva over trade policy, NATO spending, and climate commitments.
stories.watch_for: Zelenskyy is scheduled to join a working session on Ukraine aid and security guarantees. The summit concludes on June 17.

stories.headline: Dutch court sentences Syrian man to 26 years for crimes against humanity under Assad
stories.summary: A court in The Hague sentenced Rafik A., a former Assad government interrogator, to 26 years in prison for crimes against humanity committed at Syrian detention centers in 2013 and 2014, Al Jazeera reported. The court found him guilty of torture, rape, and sexual abuse of detainees; he had entered the Netherlands as an asylum seeker before his arrest in 2023. The case was the first in the Netherlands to prosecute sexual violence as a crime against humanity.
stories.context: European courts have increasingly used universal jurisdiction to prosecute former Syrian officials since the Assad government fell in December 2024. Similar cases have proceeded in Germany and France.
